title: Fresh floods, landslides disrupt normal life in several districts of Arunachal
content: At least 97,182 people have been affected by the flood in 425 villages in all 26 districts. The calamity has caused widespread damage to agriculture and public infrastructure

title: Police’s illegal entry into woman's bedroom amounts to violation of privacy: Bombay Hign Court
content: The bench directed the state to pay ₹10,000 to the 26-year-old petitioner woman within two months, clarifying that the government could recover the penalty amount directly from the errant police officer responsible

title: EC has unleashed chaos, absolute mayhem: Congress slams poll body on SIR
content: "The Election Commission has unleashed chaos and absolute mayhem that is sparing nobody," the Congress leader said

title: Which States have banned cow slaughter in India?
content: The slaughter, processing, and transport of bovine animals is governed by differing state legislation.

title: Restraining access of minors to online porn is of paramount public importance: SC
content: Supreme Court turns PIL to frame a national policy to restrict access to porn into a representation to the government; court notes petitioners’ argument that the Information Technology Act, 2000 did not penalise watching porn while punishing its production, distribution

title: Can Kerala donate an elephant to Karnataka? Elephant transfer rules explained
content: The Captive Elephant (Transfer or Transport) Rules, 2024, which were published by the Ministry on February 24, 2024, put in place the provisions that govern the transfer and transport of elephants

title: Uddhav Thackeray appeals to Sonam Wangchuk to withdraw fast
content: Uddhav Thackeray supports CJP’s protest march to Parliament on July 20, appeals to leaders from other parties including Rahul Gandhi to support the cause

title: Hyderabad SI suspended after letting six-year-old granddaughter drive, claims he was preparing her for Guinness World Record
content: Video shows the child behind the wheel on the busy Gandhamguda–Bairagiguda road as traffic piles up behind the car; the vehicle already had six pending traffic challans worth ₹3,800

title: Poompuhar to launch gifting, export initiatives; collaborate with Reliance for sale of crafts by T.N. artisans
content: Poompuhar is collaborating with Reliance Retail, which will procure handicraft products and showcase across its retail outlets. Poompuhar is making a strong push into gifting segment, with the initiative set to be launched ahead of Deepavali.

title: Honoured to receive BJP's 'love letter' in form of legal notice: Omar Abdullah
content: “I consider it a mark of respect that I am obviously a political force in Jammu and Kashmir that BJP cannot ignore," Says CM Abdullah

title: Supreme Court rejects claims of over 360 West Bengal madrasas for State salary
content: The case came up before the Supreme Court with questions including whether the madrasas in which the petitioners were appointed were validly recognised

title: Kerala HC allows KAAPA accused BJP councillor R. Sugathan to take oath in Viyyur jail
content: Sugathan is among 20 BJP councillors directed by High Court to retake oaths after they had violated procedure during original ceremony by using names of local deities. 19 councillors retook oaths, but Sugathan was detained at the time and was not able to attend the ceremony

title: Baruipur rape-murder: CPI(M) leader, held for violence, says police action 'politically motivated'
content: Mr. Ali is the first political leader to be arrested in connection with the violence that broke out in the locality in South 24 Pargana district’s Baruipur police station area on July 5

title: Retrenchments in Kerala: Labour codes and its rules | Explained
content: Chapter XI of the Code provides that the appropriate government shall set up a worker re-skilling fund, and the employer must contribute to the fund an amount equal to 15 days’ wages last drawn by the worker immediately before the retrenchment

title: Ayodhya Ram temple trust Invites applications for CEO
content: The appointment will initially be for a period of three years, and the selected candidate will be required to reside in Ayodhya during the tenure

title: India-Australia ‘administrative arrangements’ for uranium supplies | Explained
content: The recent agreement paves the way for Australian uranium exports to India, allowing private sector collaboration under stringent IAEA oversight

title: Telangana cancels car vehicle registration of vehicles driven by minors
content: Transport department cancelled 4,070 vehicle registrations across Telangana during the last financial year for allowing minors to drive

title: Three warkari pilgrims killed, four injured in road accident
content: Police are investigating the cause of the accident. In a preliminary probe, a Pune Rural official said, “It looks like a case of losing control at high speed.”

title: No caste, communal or political activities will be allowed in Tamil Nadu schools, Madras High Court told
content: Advocate General Vijay Narayan says, the aim of the School Education Department is to foster unity and togetherness among 1,03,98,748 students pursuing education in 57,671 schools across the State

title: Ayodhya Ram Temple row: Congress reiterates demand for independent inquiry, accuses BJP and RSS of ‘daylight dacoity’
content: Congress leader Pawan Khera demands a ‘complete forensic audit' of accounts, donations, offerings and land deals

title: Karnataka Governor suspends KPSC chairperson Shivashankarappa S Sahukar over 'illegal selection' of daughters in government service
content: The Governor recommended to the President that the matter be referred to the Supreme Court under Article 317(1) of the Constitution for an inquiry into the allegations

title: Pamban Bridge inauguration highlights: PM Modi inaugurates new rail bridge in Rameswaram
content: Inaugurating the new Pamban bridge in Rameswaram, PM Modi says it is the country’s first vertical lift railway sea bridge, adding efforts were on to take Tamil language, heritage to all corners of the world

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 | Kerala's football frenzy, in pictures
content: In Kerala, the FIFA World Cup brings back jerseys, late-night screenings, cutouts of star players, and celebrations every four years

title: Delayed foot overbridge on Bengaluru-Mysuru highway puts pedestrians crossing busy road at risk
content: According to Bengaluru Traffic Police data, over four fatal accidents occurred on the stretch between January 2025 and May 2026

title: Patna court grants anticipatory bail to Khan Sir, three staffers in coaching institute firing case
content: The case relates to a firing incident that occurred in early June after Mr. Khan’s coaching institute was allegedly vandalised by a group of miscreants

title: Three BJP candidates file nomination papers for Rajya Sabha byelections in Bengal
content: Three Rajya Sabha seats fell vacant after Sukhendu Sekhar Ray, Sushmita Dev and Prakash Chik Baraik resigned from the Upper House of Parliament and quit the TMC following the party’s Assembly election defeat

title: KVAFSU unveils statue of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar in campus in Bidar, holds seminar on his ideals
content: Vice-Chancellor K.C. Veeranna says Ambedkar’s ideals of equality, education and social justice remain relevant for nation-building

title: Kerala govt not seeking confrontation with HC, but needs more freedom to function: Devaswom Minister
content: Referring to Sabarimala gold theft case, K. Muraleedharan says Kerala government is unable to take any further steps as High Court had entrusted the probe to an SIT

title: Efforts on to repatriate remains of three Andhra tourists killed in Vietnam boat accident
content: The remains of Nallapeta Adiseshaiah Ravi Teja, Gelli Jaya Lakshmi and Mudium Sreedhar are to be flown home; the injured Gelli Kishore remains critical and may be moved to Ho Chi Minh City

title: BJP issues ₹100 crore defamation notice to Omar Abdullah over ‘MLA poaching’ charges
content: Calling the allegations “false, baseless and defamatory”, the BJP has demanded a written retraction and an unconditional public apology within seven days, and warned of civil and criminal proceedings if the demands are not met

title: Three publishers from Jammu, Delhi remanded in police custody for books praising separatists
content: books in question are 'Personalities and Legends of J&K', authored by Hilal Ahmad and Santosh Meena, and published by Jammu-based Oberoi Book Service, and 'Great Personalities of Jammu and Kashmir', authored by Sushant Giri and published by the Delhi-based Anurag Prakashan

title: Central Information Commission directs IIT-M to disclose utilisation of grant-in-aid funds
content: It gave the direction on a petition filed by Ananya Dubey, who sought details of the grant-in-aid received by the institute in the previous three financial years, along with project-wise utilisation of the funds, copies of utilisation certificates, and the names of collaborations

title: When anti-cow slaughter mob tried to burn down Kamaraj’s house in New Delhi in 1966
content: The Congress national president was having a siesta when the mob, taking out a rally to Parliament, attacked his residence on Jantar Mantar Road in New Delhi on November 7. The house was partly burnt. His cook, Ambi, was attacked with sticks and injured in the face. Kamaraj escaped to the adjoining Vithalbhai Hostel for MPs

title: Ban cow slaughter in Tamil Nadu with immediate effect, orders Madras High Court
content: Judges say, goats and sheep too should not be allowed to be slaughtered in any place other than a licensed slaughter house

title: Controversial Kerala YouTuber Thoppi’s channel shut down over malicious content
content: Cyber police had earlier booked Mohammad Nihad for allegedly posting obscene visuals through streaming platforms

title: Sahitya Akademi Award-winning Tamil writer Poomani passes away
content: Poomani received the Sahitya Akademi Award in 2014 for his monumental novel Agnaadi, an epic narrative set against the backdrop of the Sivakasi (1899) and Kazhugumalai (1895) communal riots; Chief Minister Vijay announces State honours

title: Supreme Court agrees to list all petitions challenging HC decision in Bhojshala case
content: The Hindu community considers Bhojshala a temple dedicated to Goddess Saraswati, while the Muslim side calls the 11th-century monument the Kamal Maula Mosque

title: Man kills girlfriend, self in Gurugram
content: An FIR of murder has been registered, and further investigation is underway, Sector-56 SHO Inspector Manoj Kumar said

title: CAG flags Kochi Corporation’s idling vehicle fleet bleeding public money
content: CAG reports finds that out of the Corporation’s 143 vehicles, 72 had to be auctioned off as unserviceable, while another 34 were off the roads for want of fitness or maintenance

title: Efforts on to fly home remains of three Andhra tourists killed in Vietnam boat accident
content: Embassy of India in Vietnam has finalised the authorised agency that will facilitate the repatriation of the mortal remains of the deceased to India

title: Naidu to launch ₹152.95-crore Cotton barrage gates renovation on July 13
content: The ₹152.95-crore renovation project is expected to be completed by July 2028; a total of 58 gates have been replaced with ₹31.77 crore during the 2014-15 financial year

title: Madras HC to take up on July 13 MRK Panneerselvam’s plea to be discharged from 2011 disproportionate assets case
content: This is the second discharge plea; the first plea was allowed by a magistrate in 2016 but reversed by the High Court in 2025

title: EPFO Amnesty Scheme 2026: What is it and who is eligible?
content: The EPFO has launched the Amnesty Scheme 2026, aimed at providing a six-month window for establishments managing Provident Fund (PF) Trusts under the Income Tax Act of 1961 to regularise their compliance status.

title: China close to cracking code of tech that controls the world - all about EUV machine
content: China has reportedly developed a functional prototype of an EUV lithography system. This development challenges Western assumptions about China's chip manufacturing capabilities. The system utilizes a mix of indigenous breakthroughs and shadow procurement networks. Experts suggest mass production could be achieved by 2030. This marks a significant shift in the global semiconductor landscape.

title: Watch: Woman slaps man sexually harassing her in MP bus
content: A man was detained after a video showed him making gestures on a bus. The viral footage sparked outrage and concerns over women's safety. Police are investigating the incident and verifying video authenticity. No formal complaint has been filed, so no FIR is registered. Strict legal action will follow if a cognizable offense is established.

title: 'We rushed Sooryavanshi': Ex-India batter says team got carried away by hype
content: Former India batter Wasim Jaffer believes Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut was premature. He felt public excitement influenced the decision to play the young player. Jaffer suggested Sooryavanshi needed more time with the national team setup. Sanju Samson's subsequent omission from the Zimbabwe tour also surprised Jaffer. Yuvraj Singh, however, expressed strong confidence in Sooryavanshi's future.

title: US job cuts hit 443,600 in 2026; this month saw the most tech layoffs
content: US companies announced 443,600 job cuts from January to June 2026.  Technology sector led these reductions, with artificial intelligence cited as the primary reason.  May 2026 saw the most severe wave of layoffs, impacting many tech firms.  AI accounted for twenty-two percent of all job cuts this year.  Hiring remains historically low, reflecting automation's impact on employment.

title: 'Putin pays attention to what PM Modi says': Top Polish official on India's role in Ukraine war
content: Poland states President Putin pays attention to Prime Minister Modi's words.  India's role is crucial in preventing further escalation of the Ukraine conflict.  President Putin praised India's independent foreign policy and its ties with Moscow.  He also described India as a trusted partner and a great nation.  Bilateral trade between Russia and India is expected to reach $100 billion.

title: 'Not Gandhiji, would kill more people': Kerala double murder convict shows no remorse in court
content: A convicted murderer showed no remorse after his guilty verdict in a sensational double murder case. Chenthamara stated he would not object to being hanged and would kill more people. He was convicted of murdering Sudhakaran and his elderly mother in Palakkad. This double murder was reportedly a revenge killing stemming from a prior case. The court will pronounce the sentence on Wednesday, concluding the trial.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the nonprofit's history. Active Minds focuses on youth mental health awareness and reducing stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support young leaders. This donation occurs amid growing concerns about youth mental health.

title: Inside Angelina Jolie’s $29.85M LA estate delaying her move abroad
content: Angelina Jolie's international move is currently stalled by her Los Feliz mansion's slow sale.  She wishes to depart Los Angeles and its memories with Brad Pitt.  Her youngest children recently turned eighteen, removing custody restrictions.  Jolie desires to close this chapter before relocating to Cambodia.  The historic estate, once owned by Cecil B. DeMille, remains on the market.

title: Meet Victoria Gowramma, Indian princess raised inside Buckingham Palace
content: The tale of Princess Gowramma, daughter of the last Raja of Kodagu, begins with her baptism in London, where Queen Victoria assumed the role of her godmother and named her Victoria Gowramma. After marrying Colonel John Campbell and having a daughter, she encountered societal bias due to her appearance. Tragically, Princess Victoria Gowramma passed away from tuberculosis, her remarkable story fading into obscurity over time.

title: TCS takes on OpenAI, Anthropic; to build a team of forward-deployed engineers
content: Tata Consultancy Services is building a large team of engineers for its AI business.  The company plans to hire up to 8,900 forward-deployed engineers to assist clients.  TCS is also exploring acquisitions in artificial intelligence and data security areas.  This strategic move positions TCS to compete directly with major AI technology firms.  The IT giant believes AI will increase the need for its integration and management services.

title: Inside Tom Holland's $4.7 M property in London: Gym, cinema, 'man cave' & more
content: During his recent trip to Mumbai for film promotions, Hollywood star Tom Holland revealed his London home, which he bought in 2018, has seen major renovations. The upgrades have significantly boosted the property's value, while the home now boasts a private gym and a luxurious home cinema. Holland values his privacy, ensuring little is known about his personal life and living arrangements.

title: Triple riding on bike doesn't automatically reduce accident compensation; Chhattisgarh HC rules
content: The Chhattisgarh High Court ruled against halving road accident compensation due to multiple passengers. The court found the victim's income was wrongly assessed at a lower amount. It increased the total compensation to nearly nineteen lakh rupees. The court also raised the consortium amount significantly for the victim's family. Respondents must pay the additional amount with interest within three months.

title: Ram Temple Trust invites application for CEO position amid donation row
content: The Ram Temple Trust is actively seeking a chief executive officer to oversee temple management. Interested candidates must submit their applications by July 18 for this key leadership position. This recruitment initiative unfolds under the Supreme Court’s investigation into alleged misappropriation of donations. The appointed CEO will be responsible for daily operations and will be based in Ayodhya, with the selection process anticipated to wrap up in the coming month.

title: CSK part ways with Stephen Fleming; Hemang Badani in fray to join set-up
content: Chennai Super Kings (CSK) have ended Stephen Fleming's stint as head coach as the franchise reviews its cricket operations after two disappointing IPL seasons. Hemang Badani is in contention to join the set-up, while CSK's leadership is also evaluating trades, the coaching staff and the captaincy ahead of IPL 2027.

title: Cody Rhodes vs Seth Rollins net worth: Who earns more in WWE?
content: Two of WWE’s biggest stars have emerged in Seth Rollins and Cody Rhodes, whose wrestling accomplishments, title wins and commercial appeal have driven interest around the world. In this comparison, the article takes a look at their projected 2026 net worth, WWE salaries, endorsements, investments, business ventures, lavish lifestyles and earning potential in the future. The article points out that both superstars made their money in different ways.

title: Fact check: Did Erling Haaland unfollow Alexander Sorloth on Instagram?
content: The claim that Erling Haaland unfollowed Alexander Sorloth on Instagram after Norway’s World Cup exit is false. The rumour came from a parody social media account after Sorloth chose not to pass to Haaland during a key attack against England. Haaland still follows his teammate on Instagram, and there is no evidence of any dispute between the two players.

title: How active stealth and electronic warfare protect the Rafale in combat
content: The Indian Air Force's Rafale fighter jet is its most advanced combat aircraft. This 4.5-generation platform offers advanced avionics and sensor fusion for pilots. It performs multiple mission types, often within a single sortie, enhancing operational flexibility. The Rafale relies on electronic means and integrated systems for radar evasion. Its survivability architecture operates in passive and active modes against hostile threats.

title: Spain's Submer to invest $2 bn in MP semiconductor sector, may create 5,000 jobs
content: Spain's Submer Group will invest two billion dollars in Madhya Pradesh's semiconductor sector. This significant investment is expected to generate approximately five thousand direct employment opportunities. The company plans to establish a manufacturing facility for advanced liquid cooling solutions. Submer aims to position India as a central hub for manufacturing and serving the Asian region. The initiative also includes skill development programs for next-generation data centers.

title: 'Actually sack those guys': Manjrekar's strong message after India's T20I drubbing
content: Former cricketer Sanjay Manjrekar criticised India's T20I series loss to England. He stated sacking players or coaches would be an easy but ineffective solution. Manjrekar believes overseas conditions and IPL preparation are the real issues. He argued IPL batting conditions mask players' true abilities abroad. The focus should be on those creating favorable IPL batting environments.

title: Dhar's Saraswati is Gayatri: How 3D mapping uncovered 900-yr mix-up, true identity of goddess
content: A 12th-century sculpture previously identified as Saraswati has been correctly identified as Gayatri. Digital documentation and 3D mapping revealed iconographic clues missed for centuries. The absence of a veena, a common Saraswati attribute, proved crucial in this re-identification. This rare image of Gayatri, embodying Vedic wisdom, was unearthed in Dhar. The rediscovered icon now represents Madhya Pradesh's digital heritage mission.

title: BrahMos at 25: How India is taking a critical missile technology into the future
content: The Brahmos missile proved decisive in Operation Sindoor, neutralising Pakistani air bases. This supersonic weapon, developed with Russian collaboration, bypasses advanced air defense systems effectively. Its deployment across all three Indian armed forces enhances strategic capabilities significantly. The missile's export success highlights its potent anti-ship and defensive platform roles. Continuous development ensures the Brahmos remains at the forefront of missile technology.

title: Meta replies to Centre's notice on Instagram ads, action likely after scrutiny: IT secretary
content: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is currently analyzing Meta's recent response to a notice regarding advertisements related to child abuse materials. The government received this reply on Saturday and is diligently reviewing its specifics. Decisions regarding future actions will be based on a thorough examination of Meta's proposed measures aimed at combating online child sexual abuse, reaffirming the government's commitment to child safety and platform accountability.

title: Watch: Puja inside moving train coach stuns social media, Railway steps in to clarify
content: A viral video showed a priest performing rituals inside a moving train coach. Northern Railway clarified the ceremony occurred in a privately booked saloon car. This luxury coach was commercially booked through IRCTC for a specific journey. The railway stated no passengers were inconvenienced by the private booking. The incident followed another video of a decorated train coach causing outrage.

title: Rowdy sheeter celebrates birthday with Karnataka BJP MLA SR Vishwanath; video goes viral
content: A video shows Yelahanka BJP MLA S.R. Vishwanath celebrating with a rowdy sheeter. The MLA reportedly felicitated Dasa, known as "King Maker Dasa", at an event. Dasa has previously been involved in controversies and police action. Police are currently collecting details and verifying the circumstances of the celebration. Further action will be decided after the investigation concludes.

title: 'Dhamaal 4' box office collection nears 70 crore on day 4
content: Indra Kumar's slapstick comedy franchise is back with its fourth installment, and 'Dhamaal 4', starring Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi, Riteish Deshmukh and Jaaved Jaaferi, has stormed into its opening weekend with strong numbers. After a Rs 65.00 crore India net in its first three days, the film enters Day 4 with early live tracking showing continued momentum, taking the total India net past Rs 65.50 crore.

title: SC stays Madras HC order directing total ban on cow slaughter during Bakrid
content: A vacation bench comprising Justice GR Swaminathan and Justice V Lakshminarayanan passed the order while hearing a public interest litigation (PIL) seeking to prevent the slaughter of cows in places other than designated slaughterhouses. "The question that calls for consideration in this PIL is whether cows and calves can be sacrificed in places not designated as slaughterhouses on the occasion of Bakrid?" the bench observed.

title: 'Jurassic Park' actor Sir Sam Neill passes at 78
content: Esteemed actor Sir Sam Neill has passed away at age seventy-eight. His family announced his sudden and unexpected demise on social media. Neill had previously battled stage three angioimmunoblastic T-cell lymphoma. He was known for his roles in Jurassic Park and Peaky Blinders. He is survived by his son and daughter.

title: Trump, Netanyahu, Meloni & more: Who is on Iran's 'hitlist' to avenge Khamenei's death?
content: Mojtaba Khamenei released a list of thirteen world leaders after his father's funeral. This publication coincided with reports of an Iranian plot against Donald Trump. US officials monitored intelligence about possible threats to the American president. The conflict continues as both sides breached a recent ceasefire agreement. Iran stated the Strait of Hormuz would remain closed until US interference ends.

title: What autopsy found after 7-year-old was raped, murdered in Ghaziabad mall
content: A seven-year-old girl was found dead with numerous injuries inside an under-construction mall. Two laborers have been arrested for allegedly luring and assaulting the child. CCTV footage helped police reconstruct the girl's final moments within the premises. The crime appears to have been planned, with the girl thrown from the third floor. Police discovered bloodstains and the iron rod used in the assault.

title: WhatsApp, SMS or email: Can your landlord legally evict you through a text?
content: To validly terminate a lease, it's essential to use the correct legal format. Informal electronic communications like SMS or WhatsApp typically lack the legal enforceability needed. However, Indian courts could accept emails if their authenticity is established. Casual messages do not meet the legal requirement for written and signed termination notices. It's crucial for landlords to adhere to these legal protocols to prevent disputes with tenants.

title: 'Citizenship process must be fair': SC relief for 27 declared foreigners in Assam
content: The Supreme Court has overturned the Gauhati High Court's rulings on foreigner status, with Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta calling for a re-examination of cases by tribunals. They highlighted that citizenship matters hold significant constitutional weight and must be approached with procedural fairness, stressing the state's duty to safeguard against misuse of citizenship assertions.

title: Global funds fall in love with Indian stocks again, buy $1 billion in equities
content: Foreign investors, who resumed buying Indian debt in June, have extended that renewed interest to equities in July. After several months of continuous selling, foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) have become net buyers this month, investing $2.59 billion (Rs 24,662 crore) during the first 10 days of July.

title: The Bengaluru Mercedes incident is just the tip of India's massive parking nightmare
content: India faces a significant civic problem with widespread illegal vehicle parking. Rapid vehicle ownership growth has outpaced parking infrastructure development across cities. Pedestrians are often forced off footpaths due to encroaching vehicles and other obstructions. Enforcement of parking rules remains inconsistent, encouraging repeat violations by drivers. Addressing this requires better infrastructure, consistent enforcement, and public cooperation.

title: Delhi Police constable shoots wife dead after heated argument
content: A Delhi Police constable allegedly shot his wife dead in Kalyanpuri on Sunday. The couple faced frequent marital discord and argued often over personal issues. CCTV footage shows them arguing heatedly on a scooter before the shooting occurred. The accused constable fled the scene and is now being searched by police. Investigators are examining footage and questioning acquaintances to understand the circumstances.

title: 'Terminator 6': Yuvraj Singh gives Vaibhav Sooryavanshi a new nickname
content: Yuvraj Singh has given Vaibhav Sooryavanshi the nickname "Terminator 6". He sees the young player as a serious talent with a bright future. Singh previously mentored Abhishek Sharma and wishes to guide Sooryavanshi too. Both players attended the Wimbledon men's singles final recently. Sooryavanshi expressed his excitement about learning from his idol Yuvraj.

title: BJP minister questions Aamir Khan's 3rd marriage; links it to 'love jihad'
content: Maharashtra minister Nitesh Rane questioned actor Aamir Khan's recent third marriage. He suggested this union could be viewed as 'love jihad'. Rane urged Hindu society to reconsider supporting such celebrities. The actor married Gauri Spratt on July fifth in Mumbai. Khan's previous marriages were to Reena Dutta and Kiran Rao.

title: Gold price prediction: Any end to gold fall in sight? Check outlook for this week
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are trading with a bearish bias as geopolitical events weigh on investor sentiment, says Manav Modi, Senior Analyst, Commodity Research at Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.

title: 'We're going to put Messi to bed': Cole's warning before England vs Argentina clash
content: England and Argentina will meet in a 2026 World Cup semifinal match. Former England player Joe Cole believes his nation can stop Lionel Messi. Both teams have battled hard to reach this crucial semifinal stage. Argentina are the defending champions and seek consecutive World Cup titles. This highly anticipated match will occur at Atlanta Stadium.

title: Five students who just made India world number 1 in Physics
content: Five Indian students—Kanishk Jain, Riddhesh Anant Bendale, Rishit Garg, Shresth Suraiya and Svarit Joshi—created history by winning gold medals at the 56th International Physics Olympiad 2026 in Colombia. Their achievement helped India rank joint World No. 1 alongside leading science nations. The feat highlights the strength of India’s Olympiad training ecosystem and showcases the advanced problem-solving, theoretical knowledge and experimental skills required to succeed at one of the world’s toughest physics competitions.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: US, Iran Both Claim Control Of Strait Of Hormuz As Tensions Escalate
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: The flare-up is the latest to undermine an interim agreement between Washington and Tehran aimed at ending their war, which has caused global economic shockwaves since it began in late February.

title: CBI Files Report, Rules Out Criminal Negligence By Officials In 2024 Coaching Centre Deaths
content: CBI's supplementary report finds negligence by 2 more MCD officials in Rau's IAS basement deaths but clears senior officers, no chargesheet filed

title: Telangana Farmers Hold 'Frog Ceremony' For Rain Amid Monsoon Deficit
content: From June 1 to July 10, Telangana recorded a seasonal rainfall deficit of about 19%, with many districts experiencing deficient rainfall.

title: Odisha Man Dies After Wife Hits Him On Head With Mobile During Fight
content: Reports suggest that an argument broke out between the husband and wife at their residence on Saturday. During the altercation, the wife allegedly hit her husband on the head with a mobile phone

title: Why It Costs More To Start A Business In India Than In China, Vietnam
content: India has roughly 6.4 crore MSMEs, but only a tiny fraction exports, despite the sector contributing nearly 45 percent of the country's exports.

title: Opinion: Opinion | India Has A New Headache: Pak's Closest Ally May Soon Get American Fighter Jets
content: In a stunning policy reversal, Trump has decided to sell America's F-35 jets to Turkey, a staunch Pakistan ally and its closest defence supplier

title: Revanth Reddy's 'Blood', 'Belt Treatment' Jibe At KCR Ignite Political Row In Telangana
content: Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy called for "belt treatment" for former Chief Minister KCR and ex-irrigation minister T Harish Rao "because they are lying."

title: Yoga Guru Ramdev's "No Need To Fear Hindu Rashtra" Comment Sparks Row
content: The 60-year-old yoga guru, who has built an empire out of mass yoga camps and ayurvedic products, claimed in a sweeping statement that "our ancestors are the same".

title: Suspended Badrinath Temple Body Staffer Arrested For Donation Irregularities
content: The Shri Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee inquiry committee found the allegations of donation mishandling against Nautiyal to be prima facie true.

title: Blocked Door, People On Fire: Witnesses Recount Bangkok Club Blaze Horror
content: Footage posted on social media showed panicked customers -- some with their clothes and hair on fire -- screaming as they fled through the flame-enveloped front door of the bar.

title: 2 Of A Family Found Dead, 2 Critical, Suicide Pact Suspected
content: Police suspect the family consumed poison as part of the alleged suicide attempt due to severe financial distress.

title: Man Who Raped, Killed 7-Year-Old In Ghaziabad Used To Play With Her
content: The girl's body was recovered during the early hours of Saturday from the basement of the building. A post-mortem examination confirmed that she was raped before her death and died due to a head injury.

title: Delhi Police Constable Shoots Wife On Road After Argument, Leaves Her To Die
content: The victim, Priyanka, was rushed to the Lal Bahadur Shastri (LBS) Hospital in East Delhi where she died during the treatment.

title: Vijay Government Secures Supreme Court Stay Against Cow Slaughter Ban Order
content: Challenging the high court's order, the state contended that when the legislation permits the slaughter of a particular category of cows in designated places, a judicial direction contradicting the statutory provision cannot be sustained.

title: India's 'Dark Data' Crisis: Why Experts Warn Of Million-Dollar Losses
content: Experts say companies will benefit more if they extract more value from existing information rather than endlessly collecting new data.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex, Nifty Trade Flat As US-Iran Tensions Escalate
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Crude oil prices jump 3% amid renewed US-Iran war tensions; Brent crude above $78 per barrel.

title: Science word of the day: Allele, its meaning, origin, applications & more
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world.
